title: result may be wrong if there's a limit without order by placed above outer join
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
create table t(a int, primary key (a));
insert into t values(1), (2);
select * from t t1 left join t t2 on t1.a=t2.a limit 1, 1;
```

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

one row output

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

empty

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

current master

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, age INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE orders(order_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER, amount REAL, FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
SELECT * FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
key_bug_features: limit without order by above outer join
